commands.repair_reason: - [P2] The remaining action is maintainer acceptance of the compatibility/security tradeoff; there is no narrow mechanical repair to queue.; Cleared: No supply-chain or permission changes were found; the diff reduces cross-origin custom-header replay by applying the existing redirect scrubber to SSE. commands.review_summary:
The PR reuses the existing redirect-safe MCP HTTP fetch wrapper for SSE request and EventSource fetch paths and adds SSE redirect regression tests.

PR surface: Source 0, Tests +148. Total +148 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main shows SSE send and EventSource fetch bypass the existing manual redirect wrapper, while the PR body gives a concrete two-origin MCP SSE proof script and after-fix output.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining action is maintainer acceptance of the compatibility/security tradeoff; there is no narrow mechanical repair to queue.

target.title: fix(mcp): align SSE redirect handling
target.body:
## Summary
- Applies the existing MCP HTTP redirect handling to SSE transport requests.
- Keeps same-origin SSE redirects compatible while avoiding replaying configured custom headers on cross-origin redirects.

## Changes
- Reuses the shared manual redirect fetch wrapper for SSE request and EventSource fetch paths.
- Adds SSE redirect regression coverage for request sends, EventSource connects, and same-origin redirects.

## Validation
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/mcp-transport.test.ts`
- `claude -p \"/review local\"` (approve)

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior addressed: MCP SSE transport follows cross-origin redirects without replaying configured custom headers to the redirect target.
- Real setup tested: Local OpenClaw checkout using `resolveMcpTransport` with the real SSE transport and two live localhost HTTP origins.
- Exact steps or command run after the patch: Ran a `node --import tsx --input-type=module` proof script that started a primary MCP SSE origin, redirected both `/sse` and `/messages` to a second origin, then called `transport.start()` and `transport.send()` through the resolved OpenClaw SSE transport.
- Evidence after fix: Console output from the live local run:

```text
OpenClaw MCP SSE redirect proof: PASS
primary origin: http://127.0.0.1:37385
redirect target origin: http://127.0.0.1:46329
SSE redirect target headers: {"accept":"text/event-stream","contentType":null,"xApiKey":null,"xTenant":null}
POST redirect target headers: {"accept":"*/*","contentType":"application/json","xApiKey":null,"xTenant":null}
```

- Observed result after fix: The cross-origin redirect target received `xApiKey:null` and `xTenant:null` on both the SSE connection and the MCP POST while safe headers such as `accept` and `content-type` remained available.
- What was not tested: No additional gaps.
target.author: mmaps

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
